difficulty.rationale: Requires opaque cursor encode/decode, forward (after) and backward (before) slicing where the backward slice is taken from the high end yet returned ascending, has_next/has_prev computed by peeking one extra row, mutual exclusion of after/before, and strict limit validation.

gates.name: security_baseline
gates.requirement: no hidden test access or network egress

must: POST /items assigns monotonic ids starting at 1.
GET /items returns items ascending by id in every mode.
after returns items with id greater than the decoded cursor.
before returns the highest-id slice below the cursor, still ascending.
has_next and has_prev reflect items beyond each returned edge.
must_not: Accept both after and before in the same request.
Accept a forged or malformed cursor.
Return a backward (before) page in descending order.
edge_cases: has_prev is false on the first forward page; has_next is false on the last page.
has_next is correct exactly at a boundary (no off-by-one).
Passing both after and before returns 400 invalid_cursor_combination.
limit <= 0 or non-integer returns 400 invalid_limit; limit > 100 is clamped.

reward_model.description: Harbor reward.txt is computed by the bun-bench gate model in tests/test.sh.
